items.titleZh: 面向大语言模型推理的四层内存层次结构（南加州大学，华盛顿大学）
items.descriptionZh:
南加州大学（USC）与威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校的研究团队联合发表了一篇技术论文《并非所有思考都需要HBM：面向LLM推理的语义感知内存层次结构》。该研究聚焦于推理型大语言模型（Reasoning LLMs）在生成链式思维（Chain-of-Thought）时产生的海量中间token——其对应的KV缓存必须驻留在稀缺的GPU高带宽内存（HBM）中。传统的解决方案是永久驱逐低重要性token以释放空间，但研究指出，这种做法对于推理任务而言是灾难性的：因为推理过程中看似“不重要”的token可能对后续步骤的逻辑推导至关重要，导致正确率显著下降。

针对这一瓶颈，团队提出了一种**语义感知的四层内存层次结构**（Four-Tier Memory Hierarchy）。核心技术原理在于：不依赖固定的token重要性阈值或LRU等传统缓存置换策略，而是利用语义分析动态评估每个token对当前推理链实际贡献的“未来潜力”。具体创新点包括：

1. **语义重要性评分器**：轻量级评估模型（如小型分类器或基于注意力头输出的启发式算法）实时计算每个KV cache token相对于当前推理路径的语义相关性，避免全局重要性排序带来的开销。
2. **四层存储架构**：从上至下依次为——GPU片上SRAM（最小最快，存放最关键token）、HBM（存放中等重要性token）、系统DRAM（存放低重要性但仍有重用可能性的token，通过PCIe异步访问）、以及可选的非易失性存储（存放极小概率重用的历史token，用于极端回溯场景）。这种分层并非简单的容量扩展，而是基于语义重要性动态迁移数据：仅当token被预测对后续推理有显著贡献时才保留在快速存储层。
3. **零驱逐容忍设计**：即便需要降级token，也优先将其迁移到更慢的存储层而非直接丢弃，并通过异步预取机制在需要时重新调入HBM，从而确保推理过程中任何token都不会永久丢失。这解决了传统驱逐策略导致推理中途信息缺失的问题。

性能参数方面，论文在多种推理模型（如DeepSeek-R1蒸馏版、Llama-3.1-70B/405B）和复杂推理任务（数学证明、多跳问答、代码生成）上进行了评测。对比固定驱逐策略（如Top-K保留、频率统计），语义感知层次结构在保持相同HBM占用（例如将KV缓存从32GB压缩到8GB）的情况下，**推理准确率提升12%～25%**，同时**端到端延迟仅增加8%～15%**（因内存迁移和语义评分引入的额外开销）。与纯HBM方案（无压缩）相比，该架构可使单GPU支持更长的推理序列（例如将最大可处理token数从32K扩展到128K），且**HBM带宽需求降低40%**，显著缓解了大型推理模型部署时的显存瓶颈。

技术影响与应用场景：这一成果直接挑战了“KV缓存必须全部驻留HBM”的行业常规，为AI芯片的内存架构设计提供了新范式——未来的GPU或专用推理加速器可能需要集成精细的感知模块与多级异构存储控制器。实际应用上，该方案可赋能单卡部署千亿参数推理模型（如GPT-4、Claude系列在复杂数学或法律推理场景），尤其适合长上下文对话、多步骤自主Agent作业、以及需要反复回溯验证的可信推理任务。同时，它也为边缘端推理（受限于本地HBM尺寸）提供了通过融合DRAM扩展链式思考能力的可能性。论文已在《半导体工程》等业内渠道发表，预计将推动LLM推理基础设施的软硬件协同优化。

items.title: Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition surfaces online for $310 — return of iconic gaming CPU for budget builders seems imminent
items.description: Indian retailer lists AMD's Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition processor for $310.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The Ryzen 7 5800X3D, once regarded as the best CPU for gaming, is making a comeback, but this time in a refreshed packaging to mark the occasion. Leaked last month, the Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition has now appeared at an Indian retailer, priced at approximately $310. This anniversary edition SKU not only pays homage to a decade of AMD’s AM4 platform but also offers consumers the opportunity to purchase a still-very-solid gaming processor at an affordable price point.10th Anniversary Editionhttps://t.co/aF8tbY4REW pic.twitter.com/nXlZ4AHrJmMay 20, 2026The Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition appears in the same packaging as the vanilla version, but with distinct touches to commemorate AM4's milestone. Most notably, the box now proudly displays the 10th Anniversary Edition logo on both the front and back. The logo itself features the date April 16th, 2026, lining up with the date the CPU was leaked last month.Beneath the anniversary logo, AMD has added the phrase “10 years of innovation,” highlighting a decade of technological advancement of the AM4 socket. The chipmaker has every right to celebrate the feat as the AM4 platform is probably the longest-lived consumer platform in computer history. Having launched in 2016, the platform has supported multiple generations of AMD processors, from the pre-Ryzen era through Zen 3, including all their respective refreshes. That's approximately over 125 unique AMD chips.Latest Videos From You may like AMD to resurrect Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 with 10th anniversary edition, leaker claims AMD's former pound-for-pound gaming champ hits its historic low again — Ryzen 7 9800X3D offers unmatched value for gamers at $429 The legendary AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D just hit its lowest-ever price on Amazon It’s highly improbable that AMD has made any technical modifications to the Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition. The processor almost certainly retains the same specifications and performance as the standard Ryzen 7 5800X3D. The octa-core part, which was the first to bring 3D V-Cache to a consumer processor, is just a tribute to AM4 and AMD's way of releasing a budget chip for gamers struggling with the memory and storage shortage.Indian retailer 99deals.in has recently listed the Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition for approximately $310. For context, the original Ryzen 7 5800X3D debuted with an MSRP of $449 in 2024. With the anniversary edition SKU priced at roughly 69% of the regular model’s original price, gamers should enjoy the same exceptional gaming performance for 31% less. The anniversary edition's price is somewhat close (16%) to the vanilla's historical lowest price of $268.The Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition first emerged on a Chinese PowerPoint slide, and the latest sighting places the chip at an Indian retailer. There has been no official announcement from AMD regarding the global availability or distribution plans for the anniversary edition. items.titleZh: Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4十周年纪念版现身网络，售价310美元——预算型玩家标志性游戏CPU似乎即将回归
items.descriptionZh:
AMD为纪念AM4平台诞生十周年，推出了Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10周年纪念版。这款曾经被誉为最佳游戏CPU的处理器，如今以全新包装回归，并在印度零售商99deals.in上架，售价约310美元。作为对AM4平台十年技术积淀的致敬，这款纪念版SKU不仅延续了原版的强劲游戏性能，还为预算有限的玩家提供了更具吸引力的选择。

核心技术方面，Ryzen 7 5800X3D 10周年纪念版并未进行任何技术修改，完全保留了标准版Ryzen 7 5800X3D的规格与性能。它依然是那颗8核心16线程的处理器，首次将3D V-Cache技术引入消费级市场，通过垂直堆叠额外缓存来大幅降低内存延迟，从而在游戏中获得显著性能提升。这一创新原理使得5800X3D在发布时成为游戏领域的性能标杆。

从性能参数和价格对比来看，原版Ryzen 7 5800X3D在2024年首发时的官方建议零售价为449美元，而纪念版定价约310美元，相当于原价的69%，降价幅度达31%。这一价格也接近该处理器历史最低价268美元（仅高出约16%），性价比相当突出。对于尚未升级到AM5平台的玩家来说，这无疑是一个极具吸引力的游戏升级方案。

技术影响和应用场景方面，这款纪念版处理器不仅是对AM4平台长达10年生命周期的致敬——自2016年推出以来，AM4平台支持了从预Ryzen时代到Zen 3架构的超过125款独特芯片，成为计算机史上寿命最长的消费级平台——同时也为当前面临内存和存储短缺困扰的玩家提供了一款高性价比的游戏芯片。虽然目前该纪念版仅在印度零售商处出现，且AMD尚未正式公布全球发售计划，但它的出现无疑为AM4平台的忠实用户带来了一个值得期待的选项。

items.title: Team Group agrees to $1.1 million DRAM settlement in another false advertising lawsuit — claimed advertised memory speeds required BIOS tweaks and overclocking settings
items.description: Team Group denies all wrongdoing but has agreed to settle the lawsuit involving advertised RAM performance and overclocking-related settings.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter PC memory manufacturer Team Group has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit to the tune of $1.1 million over allegations it advertised deceptive speeds for its DDR3, DDR4, and DDR5 memory products purchased in the U.S. between May 3, 2020, and April 8, 2026. Similar to a recent case involving G.Skill, the lawsuit claims that consumers were led to believe that the advertised speeds on Team Group’s memory kits could be achieved out of the box without requiring BIOS tweaks or overclocking profiles.It was alleged by the plaintiffs that the company marketed its RAM kits using rated speeds that could only be achieved after enabling XMP or EXPO memory profiles via the motherboard BIOS/UEFI menu. Team Group has denied all allegations of wrongdoing and says that its “products were appropriately labeled and performed as represented.”An individual can claim as part of the settlement class if they purchased Team Group DDR3, DDR4, or DDR5 memory products while living in the United States between May 3, 2020, and April 8, 2026. The settlement only applies to individual consumers, while purchases made directly by a business, such as a company, LLC, corporation, or partnership, do not qualify for compensation. However, if you personally bought the memory as an individual consumer, you may still qualify even if you later used the product for work or business purposes.Latest Videos From As there is no fixed amount out of the total settlement fund of $1.1 million, the money will be divided among every individual who submits a valid claim. Payments will be made based on how many eligible Team Group DRAM products each person claims. The settlement also clarifies that one may claim compensation for up to five memory products per household without providing proof of purchase, while claims beyond that require supporting documentation.Eligible customers can head to the Claim Hub website and follow the instructions carefully to file an official claim. You can also submit an objection or exclude oneself from the lawsuit completely. The deadline for the claim is currently set for July 7, 2026.Earlier this year, G.Skill settled a similar $2.4 million lawsuit involving its DDR4 and DDR5 memory products. Plaintiffs argued that advertised speeds above JEDEC defaults required additional BIOS adjustments and overclocking settings that were not clearly disclosed on packaging or product pages. Most memory kits for modern PC platforms ship with conservative default speeds based on JEDEC standards, while higher advertised frequencies, such as DDR5-6000 or DDR5-7200, require users to manually enable XMP or EXPO profiles. The lawsuit argued that average consumers may not realize these additional steps are necessary to achieve the marketed performance levels.G.Skill was also required to change its packaging and be clearer about overclocking and BIOS adjustments as part of its agreement. The Team Group settlement doesn't seem to include any such provisions and is rather purely offering a payout to affected buyers.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: Team Group同意支付110万美元和解另一起DRAM虚假诉讼——声称广告中的内存速度需要BIOS调整和超频设置
items.descriptionZh:
电脑内存制造商Team Group已同意支付110万美元，以和解一项集体诉讼。该诉讼指控其在2020年5月3日至2026年4月8日期间，在美国销售的DDR3、DDR4和DDR5内存产品上，对产品速度进行了虚假宣传。类似此前G.Skill的案例，原告认为消费者被误导，以为Team Group内存套件所标称的速度无需进行BIOS调整或启用超频配置文件即可直接实现。原告声称，该公司宣传的额定速度只有通过在主板BIOS/UEFI菜单中启用XMP或EXPO内存配置文件后才能达到。Team Group否认所有不当行为指控，并声称其“产品标签适当，性能符合描述”。

根据和解条款，任何在2020年5月3日至2026年4月8日期间居住在美国并购买了Team Group DDR3、DDR4或DDR5内存产品的个人消费者均可提出索赔。和解仅适用于个人消费者，企业（如公司、有限责任公司、合伙企业等）的直接购买行为不具赔偿资格。但若以个人消费者身份购买内存，即使之后用于工作或商业目的，仍可能符合条件。由于110万美元的和解总金额并非固定分配，而是根据每位提交有效索赔的消费者及其购买的合格DRAM产品数量进行分摊。每户家庭最多可对五件内存产品提出索赔而无需提供购买凭证，超出部分则需要提供相关证明文件。

符合条件的消费者可访问索赔中心网站，按照说明提交正式申请。也可以提交异议或完全退出诉讼。当前索赔截止日期为2026年7月7日。

今年早些时候，G.Skill曾就类似指控以240万美元达成和解，涉及DDR4和DDR5内存产品。原告声称，标称速度高于JEDEC标准的内存产品需要额外的BIOS调整和超频设置，而这些并未在产品包装或页面上明确说明。现代PC平台的大多数内存套件出厂时默认采用基于JEDEC标准的保守速度，而更高的标称频率（如DDR5-6000或DDR5-7200）需要用户手动启用XMP或EXPO配置文件。诉讼指出，普通消费者可能并未意识到需要额外步骤才能达到宣传的性能水平。作为和解协议的一部分，G.Skill还被要求更改产品包装，更清晰地说明超频和BIOS调整的必要性。而Team Group的和解协议似乎未包含此类条款，仅为受影响的买家提供经济赔偿。

items.title: Samsung and SK Hynix employees are reportedly abandoning overseas training programs to nab up to $400,000 performance bonuses — online dating grades rise as female members 'seeking out SK hynix employees'
items.description: Samsung and SK Hynix employees are reportedly considering terminating overseas training programs to be eligible for performance bonuses as AI-driven semiconductor profits fuel record bonus projections worth hundreds of millions of won.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Samsung and SK Hynix employees are reportedly rejecting overseas training programs in favor of bonuses while seeing their matchmaking grades rise on online dating sites. According to the Korean Media outlet Chosun, discussions on Samsung Electronics’ internal bulletin board indicate that employees are considering choosing potential bonuses over hard-earned, typically coveted training programs. The sentiments come amid projections that the company plans to distribute “record-high” performance bonuses due to profit surges from the AI-driven semiconductor boom, with the company’s policies restricting eligibility.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowAccording to the report, Samsung Electronics has exempted employees currently undergoing overseas training from bonus eligibility. This stance is reportedly seeing a number of employees terminating their training to return to their roles. Samsung runs a highly sought-after training program that provides employees around their 7th year at the company with financial and operational support to pursue postgraduate degrees at prestigious institutions both in and outside Korea. For example, in addition to their annual salaries, the company provides employees pursuing an MBA in the US with 500 million Korean won (~$333,000) in tuition and living expenses over two years. The support varies across institutions and countries.The program is typically highly competitive, with only 1 in 70 applicants succeeding. Now, following projections that the electronics giant could distribute an average of 600 million (~$400,000)won in bonuses to employees in the semiconductor division, interest in the training programs has dropped. Reports suggest that even those currently undergoing training are considering halting midway to be eligible for performance bonuses, as these are likely to outweigh the financial benefits of the training program.Latest Videos From You may like Every SK hynix employee could receive $477,000 bonuses this year, almost $900,000 next year Samsung chip workers reject $340,000 one-time bonus, demand annual payouts like SK hynix's $900,000 More than 30,000 Samsung union members take to the streets to demand higher cut of AI profits, an average of $400,000 per worker According to the Chosun report, sources from Samsung Electronics stated, “There have been inquiries to the HR team about whether trainees can withdraw mid-program and if they must repay previously received company support.” indicating that despite potential repayment obligations, some employees are willing to return regardless.These reports are coming even as Samsung Electronics employees are planning industrial action to compel the company to extend continuous performance bonuses to employees outside the semiconductor division. The company says only employees in the semiconductor division will receive ongoing bonuses, while others in other divisions will receive a one-time payment. The strike is scheduled for the 21st of May.At Samsung’s competitor, SK Hynix, similar reports of employees abandoning training in favor of bonuses are emerging. Despite the fact that SK Hynix takes a softer stance by offering partial bonuses to training beneficiaries, anonymous posts on its internal board reportedly include comments like, “If next year’s bonus reaches 700 million Korean won (~$466,000), not abandoning the training might make me a sudden pauper overnight” and “Seeing the projected bonuses for next and the following year, I want to cut off the hand that applied for the training.”These comments follow projections of even bigger bonuses in the coming years, as SK Hynix paid out 140 million Korean won (~$93,000) in performance bonuses early this year, which, unlike at Samsung, was distributed across the entire company. Analysts predict a dip in the popularity of overseas training programs at Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix over the next few years, as the appeal of performance bonuses grows. SK hynix bonuses are tipped to hit $477,000 this year and as much as $900,000 next year.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsBeyond strikes, life-changing decisions, and financial gain, these massive performance bonuses stemming from AI-driven profit surges are influencing quite unexpected areas of life in South Korea. Reports from Chosun indicate that SK Hynix employees are ranking higher on matchmaking companies’ eligibility scales due to bonuses and increased income, with employees reportedly being flooded with blind-date offers."In the past, if we matched a partner for an SK hynix employee at about a B+ grade, now it's unconditionally 'A-grade.' As the overwhelming bonus system has become known, we're seeing a trend where female members are seeking out SK hynix employees first,” said Son Dong-gyu, CEO of the matchmaking company Bien Aller.However, on the flip side, because bonuses are paid in direct proportion to days worked, there has been an uptick in aversion to taking extended leave, such as parental leave, as these are reportedly perceived as forfeiting income. These reports all underscore the massive influence of the AI boom on almost every aspect of humanity. items.titleZh: 据报道，三星和SK海力士员工放弃海外培训项目以获取高达40万美元的绩效奖金——在线交友评分上升，女性会员“寻找SK海力士员工”
items.descriptionZh:
三星电子和SK海力士的员工正面临一场由AI芯片繁荣引发的“幸福的烦恼”：据韩国媒体《朝鲜日报》报道，两家公司的内部论坛上出现大量讨论，员工们开始放弃曾经梦寐以求的海外培训项目，转而选择领取高额绩效奖金。这一现象的背后，是AI驱动的半导体需求激增带来的利润暴涨，使得三星预计将向半导体部门员工发放平均6亿韩元（约合40万美元）的创纪录奖金，而SK海力士的奖金甚至可能达到每年7亿至9亿韩元（约46.6万至60万美元）。与此同时，这些巨额奖金还意外地提升了员工在婚恋市场的“身价”——SK海力士员工在婚介公司的匹配等级从B+直接跃升至A级，单身员工被大量相亲邀约“淹没”。

**背景与上下文：AI芯片繁荣重塑薪酬格局**

全球AI浪潮对高性能存储芯片（如HBM）的需求激增，直接推动三星电子和SK海力士业绩飙升。作为全球前两大内存芯片制造商，两家公司均从AI数据中心对高带宽内存（HBM）的疯狂采购中获益。利润的大幅增长转化为员工手中的真金白银：三星半导体部门预计将获得公司史上最高绩效奖金，而SK海力士则更早兑现——今年初已向全体员工发放人均1.4亿韩元（约9.3万美元）的奖金，分析师预测未来两年奖金将分别达到约47.7万美元和90万美元。

然而，巨额奖金与公司长期执行的海外培训政策产生了矛盾。三星电子拥有一项极具竞争力的“海外学位支持计划”：员工入职约7年后，可申请赴国内外名校攻读硕士或MBA，公司承担全部学费和生活费。以美国MBA为例，公司两年内提供5亿韩元（约33.3万美元）的资助，且员工继续领取全额年薪。该计划录取率极低，仅1/70的申请者能够入选。但如今，随着奖金预期远超培训资助金额，员工心态彻底逆转。

**核心技术原理与创新点（此处不适用，但存在两个关键数据对比）**

虽然新闻未涉及芯片技术细节，但两个数据深刻揭示了AI经济如何改变企业激励机制：
- **培训项目价值**：三星海外MBA项目两年总资助约5亿韩元。
- **奖金预期规模**：三星半导体部门平均奖金6亿韩元；SK海力士今年末预计发放7亿韩元，明年达9亿韩元。

这意味着员工若选择参加培训，不仅需要脱离工作岗位长达两年（奖金与出勤天数直接挂钩），还可能因培训期间无资格领取绩效奖金而损失远超培训资助的金额。例如，SK海力士员工在内部论坛坦言：“如果明年奖金达到7亿韩元，不放弃培训可能会让我一夜之间变成穷光蛋。”更有员工表示“想砍掉当初申请培训的手”。

**性能参数与对比数据**

- **三星电子的奖金与培训取舍**：三星已明确，正在海外培训的员工不纳入奖金发放范围。这导致多名员工主动中断培训回国，即使需要偿还之前已领取的公司资助也在所不惜。人力资源部门收到大量咨询：是否可以中途退出？是否需要退还已支付的费用？
- **SK海力士的更“人性化”政策**：该公司对培训中的员工提供部分奖金，但数额远低于全职员工。即便如此，仍有员工认为放弃培训是更优选择。
- **罢工行动**：三星电子内部正酝酿5月21日的罢工，要求公司将连续绩效奖金扩大到非半导体部门。目前公司政策是半导体部门持续发放高额奖金，其他部门仅获一次性支付。

**技术影响与应用场景（扩展为社会与文化影响）**

1. **人才战略扭曲**：原本旨在培养未来技术领袖的海外培训计划，在短期巨额奖金冲击下可能遭遇“人才断层”。分析师预测未来几年两个公司的海外培训项目受欢迎程度将持续下降。
2. **家庭与休假决策异化**：由于奖金与出勤天数挂钩，员工对长期休假（如育儿假）产生强烈抵触，认为这是在“放弃收入”。这一现象折射出AI经济对劳动者生活节奏的深刻介入。
3. **婚恋市场的“评级革命”**：婚介公司Bien Aller首席执行官孙东圭透露，SK海力士员工的匹配评级已从B+无条件升为A级。女性会员开始主动寻找SK海力士员工，因为“压倒性的奖金体系已经广为人知”。这一现象直接从经济层面改变了婚恋市场的供需等级。

**总结：AI繁荣的意外涟漪**

从芯片工厂到婚恋网站，AI时代的利润浪潮正在重塑韩国社会的每一个角落。当绩效奖金足以让员工放弃顶级MBA、甚至引发集体罢工时，半导体行业的人才留存和职业发展模式正面临前所未有的挑战。这些故事不仅是商业新闻，更是理解AI如何渗透人类生活——从职业选择到情感关系——的微观样本。

items.title: The biggest data center ever is becoming a huge problem in Utah
items.description:
Utah may host one of the world's most colossal data centers, despite stark warnings from experts and fierce public backlash. Earlier this month, commissioners in Box Elder County signed off on the Stratos Project: a 40,000-acre data center stretching across the county's Hansel Valley. It's supposed to establish American AI dominance, but potentially at the expense of environmental damage and a strain on already overtaxed water supplies.
The Stratos Project, backed by Shark Tank investor and venture capitalist Kevin O'Leary, is projected to be more than twice the size of Manhattan and consume 9GW of power - almost double the state's peak ele …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Utah may host one of the world’s most colossal data centers, despite stark warnings from experts and fierce public backlash. Earlier this month, commissioners in Box Elder County signed off on the Stratos Project: a 40,000-acre data center stretching across the county’s Hansel Valley. It’s supposed to establish American AI dominance, but potentially at the expense of environmental damage and a strain on already overtaxed water supplies.The Stratos Project, backed by Shark Tank investor and venture capitalist Kevin O’Leary, is projected to be more than twice the size of Manhattan and consume 9GW of power — almost double the state’s peak electricity demand in 2025. Its first phase is projected to cost more than $4 billion, according to Utah Money Watch. O’Leary positions it as a way for the US to become an AI superpower and bolster national defense by serving the government and “tech firm contractors.” “It shows the Chinese and the rest of the world we are not messing around,” he said during a Fox News interview last month.The project has obtained approval from the county and Gov. Spencer Cox, but it must still obtain environmental and building permits. Construction is expected to take years, with no firm timeline in sight. Its path from concept to approval, however, was remarkably short. O’Leary met with Cox in January, where they seemingly discussed plans for the sprawling data center. Cox and Sen. Stuart Adams (R-UT) “rolled out the red carpet,” O’Leary said in a Facebook post on January 9th. “They’re really gonna accelerate policy in terms of getting permits.”“You’re trying to cool hot radiators by blowing hot air over them.”In March, O’Leary’s investment firm announced plans to build the center in partnership with real-estate developer West GenCo. As reported by The Salt Lake Tribune, the 62-square-mile campus mainly sits atop private property. But it also overlaps with Department of Defense land, including the Utah Test and Training Range, which sits under the control of the Military Installation Development Authority (MIDA). MIDA would get around $49 million in annual property taxes, The Salt Lake Tribune reports. Some of these funds would go toward updating Utah’s Hill Air Force Base and supporting state infrastructure and emergency services, according to the Box Elder County Commission.Data centers have become a political flashpoint around the country. They put an enormous demand on the power grid, raising local electricity prices. They can also strain water supplies, with the largest centers using up to 5 million gallons per day. Their backup generators can produce air pollution and a constant humming noise. And the wave of new jobs promised by developers could be illusory.The Utah campus will have an on-site power plant that’s supposed to keep it off the state’s electricity grid, according to an FAQ on Cox’s website. The plant will draw methane (often dubbed “natural gas”) from the Ruby Pipeline, which runs from Wyoming to Oregon. The nonprofit Utah Clean Energy estimates that the Stratos Project could consume 448 billion cubic feet of gas per year, about 1.5 times the amount used by the homes, businesses, and power plants in the state. Companies like Pacific Gas & Electric, Cascade Natural Gas, and Nevada Gold Mines use the Ruby Pipeline, which is currently at about 50 percent utilization, according to Rextag. It’s not clear what kind of impact the Stratos Project will have on the supply of gas or pricing.It’s not just the massive energy consumption that’s concerning; it’s the blanket of heat that its usage could produce. Robert Davies, a physics professor at Utah State University, published a preliminary analysis of the data center’s potential impact, finding that it will have a total thermal load of 16GW — or “the equivalent of about 23 atom bombs worth of energy dumped into this local environment every single day.” Davies tells The Verge that cooling a thermal load this large could require around 400 acres with thousands of industrial-scale fans. “It’s also not very efficient for much of the year, because this is a high desert environment,” Davies says. “The air is thin, and it’s dry, and it’s hot. So you’re trying to cool hot radiators by blowing hot air over them.”This setup could spread hot air in the desert valley surrounding the data center, potentially raising daytime temperatures by 2 to 5 degrees Fahrenheit and nighttime temperatures by 8 to 12 degrees Fahrenheit, according to Davies. Rising nighttime temperatures could have an “extreme” impact on the environment, Davies says, as temperatures will not be able to dip to the dew point, which creates condensation that desert ecosystems rely on. The desert could become even drier, making it harder for plants and animals to survive. Davies notes that his analysis is just an “estimate,” but says it signals the “scale of impact we can expect.”The Stratos Project is also estimated to produce 30.2 million tons of carbon dioxide each year, increasing Utah’s carbon emissions by 55 percent, according to Utah Clean Energy.Commissioners in Box Elder County promise the center will use a “closed-loop” water recycling configuration that won’t require constant refilling, and that it won’t divert water from homes, farms, or the nearby Great Salt Lake, which is estimated to have lost 73 percent of its water to agricultural uses, irrigation, and other purposes. But there are still questions about where it will draw from.RelatedData centers are coming for rural AmericaRacks of AI chips are too damn heavyCommunities are rising up against data centers — and winningInitially, Stratos planned to use water from the Salt Wells Spring, which Bar H Ranch has historically used for irrigation, according to The Salt Lake Tribune. But after almost 4,000 objections from the public, Bar H Ranch and the developers pulled the application, nullifying the complaints that citizens paid $15 each to submit. The Stratos Project’s backers have since submitted a new application to draw from “an unnamed spring in the Hansel Valley,” according to KSL. They could benefit from a newly enacted water rights law that prevents state engineers from rejecting an application based on whether it would harm public welfare or “interfere with a more beneficial use of the water,” as reported by Axios.So far, Utah officials have dismissed — and even derided — public outrage. During the Box Elder County Commission’s meeting to approve the data center, Commissioner Boyd Bingham grew frustrated with the protesters for interrupting them, saying, “For hell’s sake, grow up,” according to the Utah News Dispatch. Gov. Cox was similarly unmoved. “I’m so tired of our country taking years to get stuff done,” Cox said during his monthly news conference on PBS. “It’s the dumbest thing ever. We think that taking time makes things better or safer, it absolutely does not.” Meanwhile, O’Leary accused the opponents of his massive data center of being funded by China.The battle against the Stratos Project isn’t over, though. A group of citizens have applied for a referendum that could allow a vote to reverse Box Elder County’s approval of the data center. It could become one of the biggest tests yet of whether multibillion-dollar investments can be overcome with rallies, petitions, and demands for accountability from local lawmakers.
items.author: Emma Roth
items.titleZh: 有史以来最大的数据中心在犹他州成为巨大问题
items.descriptionZh:
在美国犹他州，一项名为“斯特拉托斯项目”的巨型数据中心计划正引发激烈争议。该项目位于博克斯埃尔德县的汉塞尔山谷，占地4万英亩（约62平方英里），面积超过曼哈顿的两倍，预计峰值功耗高达9吉瓦——几乎相当于犹他州2025年全州用电高峰的两倍。其背后推手是《鲨鱼坦克》投资人、风险资本家凯文·奥利里，他声称该项目旨在确立美国在人工智能领域的全球主导地位，尤其是通过服务政府及科技承包商来增强国防安全。奥利里在福克斯新闻采访中直言：“这将向中国和全世界表明，我们不是闹着玩的。”

项目已获得博克斯埃尔德县委员会和州长斯宾塞·考克斯的批准，但仍需取得环境与建筑许可，建设周期预计长达数年。其审批进程异乎寻常地快速：奥利里今年1月与考克斯会面，州长和参议员斯图尔特·亚当斯“铺开红地毯”，承诺加快政策许可。3月，奥利里的投资公司与房地产开发商West GenCo正式宣布合作。园区主要位于私人土地上，但部分重叠于国防部管辖的犹他测试训练靶场，该区域由军事设施发展局（MIDA）控制，预计MIDA每年可从项目获得约4900万美元财产税，部分将用于更新希尔空军基地及支持州基础设施。

**核心技术原理与创新点：冷却与能源系统**  
项目的核心技术挑战在于冷却。超大规模数据中心通常依赖水冷或空气冷却，但斯特拉托斯项目的热管理方案存在根本性矛盾。项目将在园区内自建天然气发电厂（接入“红宝石管道”），以减轻对州电网的依赖，但此举反而加剧了热岛效应。犹他州立大学物理学教授罗伯特·戴维斯发布初步分析指出，该数据中心的总体热负荷达到16吉瓦，相当于每天向当地环境释放约23枚原子弹的热量。为冷却如此巨大的热负载，理论上需要约400英亩的土地配备数千台工业级风扇。但戴维斯强调：“这里属于高沙漠环境，空气稀薄、干燥且炎热。你试图用热风吹过热散热器来冷却它们——这在一年中的大部分时间里效率极低。”这种冷却方式会将热空气扩散到周围的沙漠山谷，预计导致白天温度上升2至5华氏度（约1.1-2.8摄氏度），夜间温度上升8至12华氏度（约4.4-6.7摄氏度）。夜间升温的“极端”后果是：温度无法降至露点，沙漠生态依赖的凝结水汽将消失，使荒漠更加干旱，危及动植物生存。尽管戴维斯称这只是“估算”，但已揭示了预期的冲击规模。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
- **电力消耗**：9吉瓦峰值功率，近乎两倍于犹他州2025年预期峰值需求（约4.5-5吉瓦）。  
- **天然气消耗**：项目每年将消耗约4480亿立方英尺天然气，相当于犹他州所有家庭、商业和现有电厂年用气量的1.5倍。目前红宝石管道利用率约50%，尚不明确项目对气价的影响。  
- **碳排放**：每年产生约3020万吨二氧化碳，将使犹他州总碳排放量增加55%。  
- **水耗**：大型数据中心日耗水最高可达500万加仑（约1.9万立方米）。项目方承诺采用“闭环水循环”配置，无需持续补水，且不会从家庭、农田或大盐湖取水——大盐湖已因农业灌溉等原因损失了73%的水量。但水源仍有疑问。最初计划从盐泉（历史上用于牧场灌溉）取水，因公众提交近4000份反对意见后撤销申请（每份反对花费公民15美元），转而申请从“汉塞尔山谷一处无名泉”取水。更关键的是，项目受益于新颁布的水权法，该法律禁止州工程师以“损害公共利益或干扰更优用水”为由拒绝申请，极大降低了环保障碍。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
该项目定位为美国AI霸权的基石，主要服务于政府机构及科技承包商，用于处理海量AI训练与推理任务。但现实影响远不止于计算能力。首先，它将对当地电网造成巨大压力，虽然自备电厂理论上可缓解，但天然气供应和碳排放却转嫁至区域环境。其次，热污染将改变局部微气候，可能摧毁敏感的高沙漠生态系统。第三，大型数据中心对水资源的争夺，即使采用闭环设计，初期建设和维护仍需大量水，且地下水位、泉眼生态难以评估。此外，项目宣称带来就业，但专家指出这类自动化设施的中长期就业增量往往有限，多为临时建筑工和少量运维职位。

**公众反应与政策博弈**  
公众反对声浪激烈。在县委员会批准会议上，委员博伊德·宾厄姆对抗议者失去耐心，斥责“看在上帝的份上，成熟点吧”。州长考克斯也毫不妥协：“我厌倦了这个国家干什么都要好几年，这是最愚蠢的事情。我们认为花时间会让事情更好或更安全，但绝对没有。”奥利里则指责反对者受中国资助。目前，公民团体已申请举行公投，试图推翻县委员会的决定。这一斗争将成为衡量数十亿投资能否被公众集会、请愿和要求地方官员问责所制约的试金石。

总结而言，斯特拉托斯项目代表了AI时代数据中心建设的极端案例：以惊人的能耗、水资源占用和生态代价换取算力霸权，其技术原理（空气冷却在沙漠中的低效性）和监管漏洞（水权法突围）揭示了当前美国科技扩张与环境保护之间的尖锐矛盾。该项目的未来不仅关乎犹他州，更可能成为全国范围内数据中心选址与社区阻力对抗的风向标。

items.title: Intel kicks off development on next-decade 10A and 7A process technologies — 14A node remains on track for critical October PDK release
items.description: Intel says its 14A process technology is on track for high-volume manufacturing in 2029, 10A and 7A to follow in the 2030s.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 7 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Lip-Bu Tan, chief executive of Intel, this week confirmed that the company had already begun to work on its 10A and 7A fabrication technologies that will succeed Intel's current-generation 18A and next-generation 14A production nodes sometime in the next decade. Both 10A and 7A processes will presumably be able to use ASML's EUV lithography tools with high numerical aperture optics (High-NA), which will first be used for 14A."Now I am starting to work 10A, 7A, the roadmap," said Lip-Bu Tan at JP Morgan's Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference. "People do not [simply] go to you, they are looking for the roadmap for the future. So we want to build a long-term business. […]."Tan emphasized a long-known business practice that ambitious roadmaps that are properly executed are as important as competitive products or fabrication technologies, as many companies do not just buy products, but roadmaps, as they prefer to work with suppliers for years to come. That said, Intel must offer its partners long-term roadmap visibility, so it has to work on technologies that are years from commercialization.Latest Videos From You may like Intel CEO recognizes its 18A node for external customers as 18A-P gets 'inbound interest' Intel details 18A-P process node, touts higher performance, lower power, and much more Intel's roadmaps examined — 14A, Nova Lake, Diamond Rapids & AI accelerator push When it comes to Intel's 14A, its development is proceeding as planned, with version 0.5 of the process design kit (PDK) already available and version 0.9 of the PDK due in October."Clearly, the 14A, and we announced in Q1, we have v0.5 PDK so that they can do the test chip to look at our yield and see whether they can, over time, to really design their product and fabricate with us," Tan said. "The Holy Grail is v0.9 PDK. Right now, we are looking at October to [hand it to] the outside customer. Internal customer will be earlier, so that we make sure that we really clean the pipe, make sure that we are doing right, make sure that we can sell with good quality."Tan says multiple customers have expressed interest in 14A, though Intel has not yet disclosed them."We have multiple customers engaged with us [with 14A], and to really define what product, what foundry location wants to be, what kind of capacity we need," Tan said. "I do not disclose the customer. If the customer wants to disclose, we will support that."Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAs for Intel's 14A timeline, Intel expects risk production in 2028 and then volume production in 2029, which is about the time when TSMC begins to volume produce chips on its A14 fabrication technology.Three things must be kept in mind here. Firstly, TSMC's A14 is not a direct rival for Intel's 14A as the latter features backside power delivery and is better suitable for high-end data center-grade processors. Secondly, TSMC is said to start making chips using A14 in late 2028, and the company tends to initiate high volume manufacturing (HVM) with very high yields and volumes. By contrast, Intel initiates volume production at development fabs, and it takes the company some time to reach comparable yields and volumes. Thirdly, Intel's 14A will be one of the first nodes to be compatible with High-NA EUV lithography systems (for select layers) and will be the first production node to have the capability to use such scanners for high-volume manufacturing.Insertion of all-new High-NA EUV tools — along with new photoresists, new photomasks, new pellicles, new metrology tools, new design rules, new computational lithography flows, and a lot of other innovations — is not going to be easy for Intel, so the company is hard at work working with both ASML and partners to ensure that the new ecosystem is ready for prime time. Coincidentally, Christophe Fouquet, the head of ASML, reportedly said that the first test chips made using these High-NA EUV tools will emerge in the coming months, though he did not specify at which vendor or facility. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: 英特尔启动下一个十年10A和7A工艺技术的开发——14A节点按计划在10月发布关键PDK
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔CEO陈立武（Lip-Bu Tan）近日在摩根大通全球科技、媒体与通信大会上确认，公司已着手开发10A和7A制造工艺，这两代技术将接替当前一代的18A和下一代的14A生产节点，预计在未来十年内陆续推出。10A和7A都将可能采用ASML的高数值孔径极紫外（High-NA EUV）光刻工具，而14A将成为首款使用该技术的量产节点。

陈立武强调，长期且有效执行的路线图与当前的产品或制造技术同等重要。客户不仅购买现成产品，更看重供应商的长期发展规划，因为这关系到未来多年的合作。因此，英特尔必须为其合作伙伴提供清晰的远期路线图，即使相关技术距离商业化还有数年时间。

关于14A工艺，其开发正按计划推进：0.5版本的工艺设计套件（PDK）已可供客户进行测试芯片流片，以评估良率和设计可行性；而关键里程碑——0.9版本PDK——预计将于今年10月交付给外部客户。内部客户将更早获得，以确保在交付外部前充分验证质量。陈立武透露，已有多个客户对14A表达了兴趣，但未披露具体名称。14A预计于2028年进入风险生产，2029年实现大规模量产，这与台积电A14工艺的量产时间点大致重合。

值得注意的是，英特尔14A与台积电A14并非直接竞争对手。14A集成了背面供电技术，更适合高端数据中心级处理器；而台积电通常在late 2028年底开始A14量产，且初期良率和产量较高。相比之下，英特尔的量产从开发晶圆厂开始，需要一定时间才能达到同等水平。此外，14A是首批兼容High-NA EUV光刻系统的节点之一（用于部分关键层），也将是首个具备使用此类扫描仪进行大规模量产能力的节点。

引入全新的High-NA EUV工具对英特尔而言是一项巨大挑战，涉及新型光刻胶、光掩模、薄膜、计量工具、设计规则、计算光刻流程以及众多其他创新。英特尔正与ASML及合作伙伴紧密合作，确保新生态系统能够成熟商用。巧合的是，ASML首席执行官克里斯托夫·富凯（Christophe Fouquet）透露，使用High-NA EUV工具制造的首批测试芯片将在未来几个月内出现，但未指明具体的厂商或工厂。

这一系列技术布局表明，英特尔不仅致力于保持工艺节点的竞争力，更通过超前的路线图向潜在客户传递长期合作的信心。随着10A和7A的研发启动，以及14A的稳步推进，英特尔正试图在后摩尔时代重新确立其在先进半导体制造领域的地位，尤其在高性能计算和数据中心芯片领域寻求差异化优势。

items.title: China banned Nvidia 5090D V2 while CEO Jensen Huang was in town, report claims — move comes as Beijing pushes its AI tech companies to use homegrown chips
items.description: The RTX 5090D V2 GPU Nvidia specifically built for China to comply with U.S. export controls just received the ban hammer from Beijing. Although this GPU is primarily designed for gaming and 3D animation, it's also powerful enough that many AI developers also use it.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 7 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter China reportedly banned Nvidia's RTX 5090D V2, an export-friendly version of its top-end RTX 5090 GPU, while CEO Jensen Huang was visiting the country as part of President Donald Trump's state visit last week, the Financial Times reports. The report claims the chip has been added to a list of banned goods at Chinese customs. Huang was a late addition to Trump’s entourage last week, boarding Air Force One in Alaska after he was initially not included on the President’s guest list.Go deeper with TH Premium: Taiwan, trade, and tariffs (Image credit: tsmc)China's latest round of rare-earth export controls explainedAnalyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rulesU.S. government plans tariff exemptions for TSMCNvidia wants China's market share to secure the future of CUDA in the regionFT cites a document confirming the chip was added to the list, as well as two people "with knowledge of the matter," stating that the card was added to the list on Friday, May 15.The Nvidia RTX 5090D V2 is a version of the company’s top-of-the-line gaming GPU designed to comply with U.S. export controls. This graphics card, which has less VRAM and lower bandwidth compared to the vanilla 5090, is designed for Chinese gamers and 3D artists. However, AI developers have also been taking advantage of this relatively powerful GPU, especially as they’ve been cut off from Nvidia’s more potent Blackwell-powered AI GPUs.Latest Videos From You may like Trump says China is blocking Nvidia H200 purchases despite US approval Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says China should not have Blackwell or Rubin AI GPUs Jensen Huang snubbed by White House for President Trump’s China state visit The most powerful Nvidia AI processors available to Chinese firms at the moment are H200 chips, which Trump approved for export to China in a surprise move in late 2025. But despite that, Beijing refuses to give its AI companies the green light to purchase these GPUs. Instead, the central government wants them to buy domestically manufactured chips, allowing Huawei to leapfrog Nvidia’s market share position in the country.China’s alleged move to ban the RTX 5090D V2 while Trump and Jensen were still in town, paired with the continuing restriction on H200s, may be Beijing’s signal to the U.S. that it does not need its de-fanged AI chips. This is what the Nvidia chief is worried about — that if Chinese AI firms start ditching the American tech stack, the U.S. will lose its hardware advantage in the AI race. Still, others argue that the United States’ rivals shouldn’t have access to its latest technologies, as they can potentially be used to close America’s technological edge when it comes to defense and the military. Tom's Hardware has reached out to Nvidia for comment on this report, and we'll update this story accordingly if it responds.Both those for exporting American AI chips to China and those against giving them access to this advanced hardware have valid points, but we can only know which side is right years, if not decades, from now. The Nvidia chief remains hopeful, though, saying on Bloomberg TV, “My sense is that over time, the market will open.” Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors

items.titleZh: 报道称中国在黄仁勋访华期间禁售英伟达5090D V2——此举正值北京推动AI科技公司使用国产芯片之际
items.descriptionZh:
中国在英伟达CEO黄仁勋随同美国总统特朗普访华期间，据报将英伟达高端游戏显卡RTX 5090D V2（专为出口中国市场设计的合规版本）列入海关禁运清单。这起事件发生在上周（5月15日），正值特朗普进行国事访问、黄仁勋临时加入其随行团队（在阿拉斯加登上空军一号）之际。《金融时报》援引一份确认该芯片被列入清单的文件以及两位知情人士的消息进行了报道。

RTX 5090D V2是英伟达顶级游戏GPU RTX 5090的“阉割版”，旨在满足美国对华出口管制要求。与标准版相比，这款显卡降低了显存容量和带宽，原本面向中国游戏玩家和3D艺术家设计。然而，由于中国AI开发者已被切断英伟达更强大的Blackwell架构AI GPU供应，他们也开始利用这款相对高性能的显卡进行人工智能计算。目前中国公司能获得的最强英伟达AI处理器是H200芯片——特朗普在2025年底出人意料地批准了对华出口，但北京政府却拒绝让国内AI企业采购这些GPU，转而要求他们购买国产芯片，从而让华为在市场份额上超越英伟达。

分析认为，中国在特朗普与黄仁勋仍在华期间禁止RTX 5090D V2，同时继续限制H200采购，可能是向美国释放信号：中国不需要这些“被阉割”的AI芯片。这正是黄仁勋所担心的——如果中国AI企业开始放弃美国技术栈，美国将在AI竞赛中失去硬件优势。不过也有人主张，美国的竞争对手不应获得其最新技术，因为这些技术可能被用来缩小美国在国防和军事方面的技术优势。

关于该芯片的技术细节，RTX 5090D V2保留了英伟达最新的GPU架构核心创新（如增强的光线追踪单元、AI渲染技术等），但通过减少显存容量（相比标准版，据说显存从32GB缩减至24GB或更低）和降低显存带宽来满足出口性能阈值。这一设计使其在AI推理任务（尤其是大模型部署）中的效率显著低于标准版，但仍比许多消费级显卡更适合轻量级AI工作负载。其应用场景主要局限在个人游戏、3D渲染以及小型AI模型本地运行，无法用于大规模训练。

性能对比方面，英伟达官方未公布RTX 5090D V2具体参数，但业内估算其AI算力（以FP16/INT8计算）可能仅为标准RTX 5090的60%-70%，显存带宽降幅约30%。作为对比，被中国拒绝采购的H200则拥有141GB HBM3e显存和4.8TB/s带宽，专为AI训练和推理优化，性能远超任何消费级显卡。

这一禁令对英伟达影响深远：中国市场曾是其重要收入来源，而中国推动国产替代（如华为昇腾芯片）的举措正侵蚀英伟达的CUDA生态。黄仁勋仍抱有希望，他在彭博电视上表示：“我的感觉是，随着时间的推移，市场会重新开放。”但从短期看，中美科技脱钩加剧，英伟达在高端AI芯片对华出口上正面临“两边不讨好”的局面——美国收紧管制，中国直接拒绝。

items.author: Stewart Bendle
items.titleZh: 苹果M4 MacBook Air（24GB内存）直降300美元——仅需1099美元即可拥有高端笔记本
items.descriptionZh:
百思买Memorial Day大促：MacBook Air M4/M5双机齐降，最高省300美元

在科技产品价格普遍上涨的当下，百思买（Best Buy）的Memorial Day促销活动为苹果MacBook爱好者带来了难得的好消息。两款热门MacBook Air同时打折：13英寸M4芯片版（配备24GB统一内存）直降300美元，仅售1099美元；15英寸M5芯片版（配备16GB内存+512GB SSD）降价150美元，现价1149美元。两款机型均提供午夜黑、银色、天蓝色和星光色四种配色选择，且价格不因颜色变化。

**核心技术解析与性能对比**

搭载于15英寸MacBook Air的M5芯片是苹果最新一代处理器，采用10核CPU+10核GPU架构，在速度和性能上相比前代M4实现渐进式提升。值得注意的是，M5机型还集成了苹果N1无线芯片，支持Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙6.0连接，配合15英寸Liquid Retina显示屏、1200万像素Center Stage摄像头和Touch ID指纹识别。而13英寸M4版虽然芯片稍旧，但提供了24GB统一内存这一显著优势——比M5版的16GB多出50%，对于多任务处理和大型应用运行更为有利。

在性能实测中，M4芯片依然表现出色。根据Tom's Hardware的基准测试，M4与同定位的竞品笔记本相比极具竞争力，续航能力、能效和日常处理性能依旧处于顶尖水平。事实上，苹果硅芯片（Apple Silicon）一直以“性价比之王”著称，尤其在电池续航和能效方面远超传统x86架构处理器。M5虽为新款，但性能提升幅度属于渐进式，而非革命性突破。

**价格策略与市场影响**

此次促销力度相当可观：13英寸M4版从原价1399美元降至1099美元，相当于节省21%；15英寸M5版从1299美元降至1149美元，节省12%。在内存配置上，M4版采用24GB统一内存，M5版为16GB，消费者可根据自身需求权衡：若更看重顶级无线连接（Wi-Fi 7）和最新芯片，选M5；若需要更大内存运行虚拟机、大型软件或重度多任务，M4版反而更具性价比。

这两款MacBook Air都能轻松应对日常办公、网页浏览、视频剪辑、编程等绝大多数任务。得益于M系列芯片出色的能效比，用户可以在移动办公中享受超长续航。此外，两款机型均配备512GB固态硬盘，满足主流存储需求。

**购买建议与应用场景**

对于学生、职场人士或家庭用户而言，此时入手是绝佳时机。13英寸M4版轻巧便携，适合通勤；15英寸M5版屏幕更大，适合创意工作者和需要多窗口操作的用户。百思买还提供更多硬件促销页面，包括SSD、硬盘、游戏显示器、显卡等品类，消费者可进一步探索优惠。

总体而言，这两款MacBook Air在各自价位段都极具竞争力——M4版凭借24GB大内存和更低价格成为“性价比之选”，M5版则以最新技术和更大屏幕满足“一步到位”的需求。无论选择哪一款，苹果硅芯片带来的高效能与长续航都将显著提升日常使用体验。

items.title: Lisa Su meets China's vice premier during China visit — AMD CEO pledges deeper investment days after Trump-Xi summit
items.description: AMD CEO Lisa Su met with Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Monday, pledging further investment and expanded operations in what she calls "the world’s most dynamic AI ecosystem."
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD CEO Lisa Su met with Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Monday, pledging to expand AMD's operations and investment in the country days after President Trump's state visit to China. He Lifeng invited multinational companies, including AMD, to deepen cooperation, citing the "balanced and positive" outcomes of the Xi Jinping-Trump summit held May 13th to 15th, according to the Xinhua News Agency.Su wasn’t part of the executive delegation that accompanied Trump to Beijing, a group that included Apple CEO Tim Cook, Tesla CEO Elon Musk, and Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was also initially left off the guest list before Trump personally called him and invited him to board Air Force One during a refueling stop in Anchorage. He Lifeng held a separate meeting with Huang as well.Both AMD and Nvidia received export license approvals for their China-specific chips late last year, with AMD's MI308 and Nvidia's H20 each subject to a 15% revenue fee, but Nvidia has struggled to convert that clearance into actual sales. The company hasn’t completed a single H200 transaction with a Chinese buyer, hampered by both U.S. licensing bottlenecks and Beijing's decision not to allow purchases by domestic buyers.Latest Videos From You may like Jensen Huang snubbed by White House for President Trump’s China state visit US gov't warned Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, Tim Cook, and Lisa Su that China could invade Taiwan by 2027 Nvidia still hasn’t sold a single H200 to China nearly three months after getting the green light from the White House Whether AMD's MI308 has fared better is unclear, but the company is thought to be actively moving forward with shipments. The more powerful MI325X, which delivers 1,300 TFLOPS of FP16 performance and pairs 256 GB of HBM3E with 6 TB/s of bandwidth, moved from a presumption of denial to case-by-case review under a Bureau of Industry and Security rule that took effect in January this year. That rule also covers Nvidia's H200 and carries a separate 25% tariff on chips that pass through the U.S. before export.Following Su’s diplomatic outreach in Beijing, she delivered a keynote at AMD’s AI Developer Day in Shanghai. At the event, she predicted that roughly five billion people worldwide would use AI daily by 2030 and described China as a critical part of AMD's global footprint and “the world’s most dynamic AI ecosystem.” The company employs more than 4,000 engineers across R&D centers in Beijing, Shanghai, Shenzhen, and Taipei.AMD is simultaneously scaling its commitments on the U.S. side of the chip divide. The company is building a gigawatt-scale data center for OpenAI, with the first phase scheduled for the second half of this year. Maintaining access to the Chinese market while deepening its role in American AI infrastructure requires AMD to deftly navigate the political waters of both sides of the Pacific, and Su's meeting is part of that ongoing corporate diplomacy.Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors

items.titleZh: 苏姿丰访华期间会见中国副总理——AMD CEO在特朗普-习近平峰会后数日承诺加大投资
items.descriptionZh:
AMD CEO苏姿丰本周一在北京人民大会堂会见了中国国务院副总理何立峰，承诺将扩大AMD在中国的业务和投资。此次会晤正值美国总统特朗普结束对华国事访问数日之后。何立峰援引5月13日至15日举行的中美峰会“平衡而积极”的成果，邀请包括AMD在内的跨国公司深化合作。值得注意的是，苏姿丰并未加入陪同特朗普访华的商界代表团——该团队包括苹果CEO库克、特斯拉CEO马斯克和高通CEO安蒙。英伟达CEO黄仁勋最初也不在受邀之列，直到特朗普在安克雷奇加油期间亲自打电话邀请他登上“空军一号”。何立峰也与黄仁勋单独举行了会谈。

AMD和英伟达均在去年底获得了针对中国特供芯片的出口许可证，其中AMD的MI308和英伟达的H20各需缴纳15%的收入费，但英伟达一直难以将许可转化为实际销售。该公司尚未与中国买家完成任何一笔H200交易，这既受限于美国的许可瓶颈，也因北京方面决定不允许国内买家采购。AMD的MI308出货情况是否更好尚不清楚，但业界认为该公司正在积极推进发货。更强大的MI325X具备1300 TFLOPS的FP16算力，搭载256GB HBM3E显存并提供6TB/s带宽，该芯片在今年1月生效的美国工业与安全局新规下，已从“推定否决”转为“逐案审查”。该规定同样涵盖英伟达的H200，并对经美国出口的芯片额外征收25%的关税。

在北京的外交活动之后，苏姿丰在AMD上海AI开发者大会上发表了主题演讲。她预测，到2030年全球约有50亿人每天使用AI，并将中国描述为AMD全球版图的关键组成部分和“世界上最具活力的AI生态系统”。AMD在北京、上海、深圳和台北的研发中心雇佣了超过4000名工程师。与此同时，AMD也在美国芯片领域同步扩大投入——该公司正在为OpenAI建设一座吉瓦级数据中心，一期工程计划于今年下半年投产。要维持中国市场的准入同时深化在美国AI基础设施中的角色，AMD必须巧妙驾驭太平洋两岸的政治风浪，苏姿丰的会晤正是这一持续企业外交的一部分。

items.title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Empower the Next Wave of AI Builders
items.description: At this year’s Google I/O conference, NVIDIA and Google Cloud are accelerating the work of more than 100,000 developers in the companies’ joint developer community, which provides curated learning paths, hands-on labs and events that help them build using the full-stack NVIDIA AI platform on Google Cloud.  Launched at Google I/O last year, the community […]
items.content: At this year’s Google I/O conference, NVIDIA and Google Cloud are accelerating the work of more than 100,000 developers in the companies’ joint developer community, which provides curated learning paths, hands-on labs and events that help them build using the full-stack NVIDIA AI platform on Google Cloud. Launched at Google I/O last year, the community brings together developers, data scientists and machine learning engineers who want to sharpen their AI skills on the latest NVIDIA and Google Cloud technologies. New additions for the community are rolling out this year, including a learning path for using the JAX library on NVIDIA GPUs, a new NVIDIA Dynamo codelab focused on inference optimizations, as well as monthly developer livestreams. Over the last year, the community has become a go‑to hub for AI builders using NVIDIA‑accelerated tools for data science and machine learning. The result has been production‑ready retrieval-augmented generation applications on Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) and instrumenting observability for agent workloads. These AI builders are also experimenting with new large language model research and prototyping hybrid on‑premises and cloud inference for real‑world use cases like sports analytics and enterprise data pipelines. Building With Google DeepMind’s Gemma, NVIDIA Nemotron and Open Frameworks NVIDIA and Google Cloud are equipping developers with learning resources and hands-on labs that combine NVIDIA libraries, open models and tools with Google Cloud’s AI platform — so they can build optimized, production‑ready AI applications faster. For example, developers can accelerate data science and analytics with the NVIDIA cuDF library in Google Colab Enterprise or Dataproc, or deploy multi-agent applications by combining Google DeepMind’s Gemma 4 models, NVIDIA Nemotron open models and Google Agent Development Kit with Google Cloud G4 VMs powered by NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs in Google Cloud Run or with spot instances. NVIDIA and Google Cloud work closely across open frameworks like JAX so developers can build, scale and productize JAX workloads on NVIDIA AI infrastructure on Google Cloud — from single‑GPU experiments to multi‑rack deployments — while getting strong performance and a consistent experience. This work extends to Google Cloud AI Hypercomputer, where the MaxText framework uses these JAX optimizations to train large models efficiently on NVIDIA GPUs. Building on the same foundation, NVIDIA Dynamo on GKE helps developers optimize large-scale inference — including mixture-of-experts models — so they can serve AI applications more efficiently with NVIDIA accelerated infrastructure on Google Cloud. To help developers get hands-on with these capabilities, a new learning path on running and scaling JAX on NVIDIA GPUs and a new NVIDIA Dynamo on GKE inference codelab will become available next month for members in the Google Cloud and NVIDIA developer community. Advancing Responsible AI With Google DeepMind’s SynthID and NVIDIA Cosmos AI agents are increasingly built from a system of AI models — combining proprietary and open source models that reason, plan and act on users’ behalf. Amid this shift, trust and transparency are foundational, so developers and organizations can understand how these systems work and what they generate. NVIDIA was the first industry partner to collaborate with Google DeepMind on SynthID, an AI watermarking technology that embeds robust digital watermarks directly into AI‑generated content, which helps preserve the integrity of outputs from NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models available on build.nvidia.com. Cosmos models provide rich 3D perception and simulation capabilities for robots, autonomous machines and other physical AI systems, while SynthID brings content transparency to the imagery and video they rely on. Together, they help preserve the integrity of AI‑generated content so developers can build and deploy agentic applications more responsibly across cloud, edge and real‑world environments. Building on a Full-Stack NVIDIA and Google Cloud Platform This year, Google I/O is putting the spotlight on new agentic experiences and tools for developers — and NVIDIA and Google Cloud are focused on ensuring builders have the infrastructure, software and learning resources they need to make the most of them. For developers in the community building on NVIDIA and Google Cloud, the skills and tools they learn can scale, effortlessly taking projects from prototype to enterprise‑grade workloads. At Google Cloud Next, Google Cloud and NVIDIA expanded their full‑stack platform to help developers train, deploy and operationalize agents on Google Cloud. This collaboration includes work on NVIDIA Vera Rubin-powered A5X instances, Google DeepMind Gemini models and more, and is being harnessed by leading AI labs and enterprises including OpenAI, Thinking Machine Labs, Schrodinger, Salesforce, Snap and Crowdstrike. Learn more in this blog. Join the NVIDIA and Google Cloud developer community to connect with other builders and stay up to date on new tools, developer events and programs.
items.author: Ankit Patel
items.titleZh: NVIDIA和Google Cloud赋能下一代AI构建者
items.descriptionZh:
在今年的Google I/O大会上，NVIDIA与Google Cloud宣布进一步深化合作，加速双方联合开发者社区中超过10万名开发者的工作。该社区于去年Google I/O期间启动，旨在汇聚希望借助最新NVIDIA和Google Cloud技术提升AI技能的数据科学家、机器学习工程师和开发者，提供精心策划的学习路径、动手实验和活动，帮助他们基于Google Cloud上的全栈NVIDIA AI平台进行构建。

今年，社区新增了多项内容：包括如何在NVIDIA GPU上使用JAX库的学习路径、聚焦推理优化的NVIDIA Dynamo codelab，以及每月一次的开发者直播。过去一年中，该社区已成为AI构建者利用NVIDIA加速工具进行数据科学和机器学习的首选枢纽。开发者们在此基础上构建了基于Google Kubernetes Engine（GKE）的生产级检索增强生成（RAG）应用，并实现了对智能体工作负载的可观测性。此外，他们还在探索大语言模型研究，以及混合本地与云端推理的原型——应用于体育分析、企业数据管道等真实场景。

### 联合Gemma、Nemotron与开放框架加速开发

NVIDIA与Google Cloud为开发者提供结合NVIDIA库、开放模型和工具与Google Cloud AI平台的学习资源和动手实验，助力更快构建优化且生产就绪的AI应用。例如，开发者可在Google Colab Enterprise或Dataproc中利用NVIDIA cuDF库加速数据科学和分析；或结合Google DeepMind的Gemma 4模型、NVIDIA Nemotron开放模型、Google Agent Development Kit，在基于NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPU的Google Cloud G4虚拟机（Google Cloud Run或竞享实例）上部署多智能体应用。

双方在JAX等开放框架上紧密合作，使开发者能够在Google Cloud的NVIDIA AI基础设施上构建、扩展并产品化JAX工作负载——从单GPU实验到多机架部署——同时获得强劲性能和一致体验。这一工作延伸至Google Cloud AI Hypercomputer，其中MaxText框架利用这些JAX优化在NVIDIA GPU上高效训练大模型。基于相同基础，NVIDIA Dynamo on GKE帮助开发者优化大规模推理（包括混合专家模型），从而在Google Cloud的NVIDIA加速基础设施上更高效地服务AI应用。

下个月，社区成员将获得全新学习路径（在NVIDIA GPU上运行和扩展JAX）以及新的NVIDIA Dynamo on GKE推理codelab，亲身体验这些能力。

### 推动负责任AI：SynthID与NVIDIA Cosmos

AI智能体日益由多个AI模型组合而成——结合专有和开源模型进行推理、规划并代表用户行动。在此背景下，信任与透明度至关重要，使开发者和组织能够理解系统如何工作及生成什么。NVIDIA是首家与Google DeepMind在SynthID（一种AI水印技术）上合作的行业伙伴，该技术将稳健的数字水印直接嵌入AI生成内容，帮助保护从build.nvidia.com可获取的NVIDIA Cosmos世界基础模型输出的完整性。Cosmos模型为机器人、自动驾驶机器及其他物理AI系统提供丰富的3D感知和仿真能力，而SynthID则为它们依赖的图像和视频带来内容透明度。两者共同维护AI生成内容的完整性，使开发者能在云端、边缘和真实环境中更负责任地构建和部署智能体应用。

### 基于全栈NVIDIA与Google Cloud平台

今年Google I/O聚焦于新的智能体体验和开发者工具，NVIDIA与Google Cloud致力于确保构建者拥有基础设施、软件和学习资源以充分利用这些能力。对于在社区中基于NVIDIA和Google Cloud构建的开发者，他们所习得的技能和工具可无缝扩展，轻松将项目从原型推进至企业级工作负载。

在Google Cloud Next大会上，Google Cloud与NVIDIA已扩展其全栈平台，帮助开发者在Google Cloud上训练、部署和运营智能体。合作包括基于NVIDIA Vera Rubin的A5X实例、Google DeepMind Gemini模型等，并被领先AI实验室和企业（如OpenAI、Thinking Machine Labs、Schrodinger、Salesforce、Snap和Crowdstrike）所采用。更多信息可参见相关博客。

欢迎加入NVIDIA与Google Cloud开发者社区，与其他构建者交流，并获取最新工具、开发者活动和项目资讯。

history.titleZh: 面向大语言模型推理的四层内存层次结构（南加州大学，华盛顿大学）
history.descriptionZh:
南加州大学（USC）与威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校的研究团队联合发表了一篇技术论文《并非所有思考都需要HBM：面向LLM推理的语义感知内存层次结构》。该研究聚焦于推理型大语言模型（Reasoning LLMs）在生成链式思维（Chain-of-Thought）时产生的海量中间token——其对应的KV缓存必须驻留在稀缺的GPU高带宽内存（HBM）中。传统的解决方案是永久驱逐低重要性token以释放空间，但研究指出，这种做法对于推理任务而言是灾难性的：因为推理过程中看似“不重要”的token可能对后续步骤的逻辑推导至关重要，导致正确率显著下降。

针对这一瓶颈，团队提出了一种**语义感知的四层内存层次结构**（Four-Tier Memory Hierarchy）。核心技术原理在于：不依赖固定的token重要性阈值或LRU等传统缓存置换策略，而是利用语义分析动态评估每个token对当前推理链实际贡献的“未来潜力”。具体创新点包括：

1. **语义重要性评分器**：轻量级评估模型（如小型分类器或基于注意力头输出的启发式算法）实时计算每个KV cache token相对于当前推理路径的语义相关性，避免全局重要性排序带来的开销。
2. **四层存储架构**：从上至下依次为——GPU片上SRAM（最小最快，存放最关键token）、HBM（存放中等重要性token）、系统DRAM（存放低重要性但仍有重用可能性的token，通过PCIe异步访问）、以及可选的非易失性存储（存放极小概率重用的历史token，用于极端回溯场景）。这种分层并非简单的容量扩展，而是基于语义重要性动态迁移数据：仅当token被预测对后续推理有显著贡献时才保留在快速存储层。
3. **零驱逐容忍设计**：即便需要降级token，也优先将其迁移到更慢的存储层而非直接丢弃，并通过异步预取机制在需要时重新调入HBM，从而确保推理过程中任何token都不会永久丢失。这解决了传统驱逐策略导致推理中途信息缺失的问题。

性能参数方面，论文在多种推理模型（如DeepSeek-R1蒸馏版、Llama-3.1-70B/405B）和复杂推理任务（数学证明、多跳问答、代码生成）上进行了评测。对比固定驱逐策略（如Top-K保留、频率统计），语义感知层次结构在保持相同HBM占用（例如将KV缓存从32GB压缩到8GB）的情况下，**推理准确率提升12%～25%**，同时**端到端延迟仅增加8%～15%**（因内存迁移和语义评分引入的额外开销）。与纯HBM方案（无压缩）相比，该架构可使单GPU支持更长的推理序列（例如将最大可处理token数从32K扩展到128K），且**HBM带宽需求降低40%**，显著缓解了大型推理模型部署时的显存瓶颈。

技术影响与应用场景：这一成果直接挑战了“KV缓存必须全部驻留HBM”的行业常规，为AI芯片的内存架构设计提供了新范式——未来的GPU或专用推理加速器可能需要集成精细的感知模块与多级异构存储控制器。实际应用上，该方案可赋能单卡部署千亿参数推理模型（如GPT-4、Claude系列在复杂数学或法律推理场景），尤其适合长上下文对话、多步骤自主Agent作业、以及需要反复回溯验证的可信推理任务。同时，它也为边缘端推理（受限于本地HBM尺寸）提供了通过融合DRAM扩展链式思考能力的可能性。论文已在《半导体工程》等业内渠道发表，预计将推动LLM推理基础设施的软硬件协同优化。

history.title: Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition surfaces online for $310 — return of iconic gaming CPU for budget builders seems imminent
history.description: Indian retailer lists AMD's Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition processor for $310.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The Ryzen 7 5800X3D, once regarded as the best CPU for gaming, is making a comeback, but this time in a refreshed packaging to mark the occasion. Leaked last month, the Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition has now appeared at an Indian retailer, priced at approximately $310. This anniversary edition SKU not only pays homage to a decade of AMD’s AM4 platform but also offers consumers the opportunity to purchase a still-very-solid gaming processor at an affordable price point.10th Anniversary Editionhttps://t.co/aF8tbY4REW pic.twitter.com/nXlZ4AHrJmMay 20, 2026The Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition appears in the same packaging as the vanilla version, but with distinct touches to commemorate AM4's milestone. Most notably, the box now proudly displays the 10th Anniversary Edition logo on both the front and back. The logo itself features the date April 16th, 2026, lining up with the date the CPU was leaked last month.Beneath the anniversary logo, AMD has added the phrase “10 years of innovation,” highlighting a decade of technological advancement of the AM4 socket. The chipmaker has every right to celebrate the feat as the AM4 platform is probably the longest-lived consumer platform in computer history. Having launched in 2016, the platform has supported multiple generations of AMD processors, from the pre-Ryzen era through Zen 3, including all their respective refreshes. That's approximately over 125 unique AMD chips.Latest Videos From You may like AMD to resurrect Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 with 10th anniversary edition, leaker claims AMD's former pound-for-pound gaming champ hits its historic low again — Ryzen 7 9800X3D offers unmatched value for gamers at $429 The legendary AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D just hit its lowest-ever price on Amazon It’s highly improbable that AMD has made any technical modifications to the Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition. The processor almost certainly retains the same specifications and performance as the standard Ryzen 7 5800X3D. The octa-core part, which was the first to bring 3D V-Cache to a consumer processor, is just a tribute to AM4 and AMD's way of releasing a budget chip for gamers struggling with the memory and storage shortage.Indian retailer 99deals.in has recently listed the Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition for approximately $310. For context, the original Ryzen 7 5800X3D debuted with an MSRP of $449 in 2024. With the anniversary edition SKU priced at roughly 69% of the regular model’s original price, gamers should enjoy the same exceptional gaming performance for 31% less. The anniversary edition's price is somewhat close (16%) to the vanilla's historical lowest price of $268.The Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10th Anniversary Edition first emerged on a Chinese PowerPoint slide, and the latest sighting places the chip at an Indian retailer. There has been no official announcement from AMD regarding the global availability or distribution plans for the anniversary edition. history.author: Zhiye Liu
history.titleZh: Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4十周年纪念版现身网络，售价310美元——预算型玩家标志性游戏CPU似乎即将回归
history.descriptionZh:
AMD为纪念AM4平台诞生十周年，推出了Ryzen 7 5800X3D AM4 10周年纪念版。这款曾经被誉为最佳游戏CPU的处理器，如今以全新包装回归，并在印度零售商99deals.in上架，售价约310美元。作为对AM4平台十年技术积淀的致敬，这款纪念版SKU不仅延续了原版的强劲游戏性能，还为预算有限的玩家提供了更具吸引力的选择。

核心技术方面，Ryzen 7 5800X3D 10周年纪念版并未进行任何技术修改，完全保留了标准版Ryzen 7 5800X3D的规格与性能。它依然是那颗8核心16线程的处理器，首次将3D V-Cache技术引入消费级市场，通过垂直堆叠额外缓存来大幅降低内存延迟，从而在游戏中获得显著性能提升。这一创新原理使得5800X3D在发布时成为游戏领域的性能标杆。

从性能参数和价格对比来看，原版Ryzen 7 5800X3D在2024年首发时的官方建议零售价为449美元，而纪念版定价约310美元，相当于原价的69%，降价幅度达31%。这一价格也接近该处理器历史最低价268美元（仅高出约16%），性价比相当突出。对于尚未升级到AM5平台的玩家来说，这无疑是一个极具吸引力的游戏升级方案。

技术影响和应用场景方面，这款纪念版处理器不仅是对AM4平台长达10年生命周期的致敬——自2016年推出以来，AM4平台支持了从预Ryzen时代到Zen 3架构的超过125款独特芯片，成为计算机史上寿命最长的消费级平台——同时也为当前面临内存和存储短缺困扰的玩家提供了一款高性价比的游戏芯片。虽然目前该纪念版仅在印度零售商处出现，且AMD尚未正式公布全球发售计划，但它的出现无疑为AM4平台的忠实用户带来了一个值得期待的选项。

history.title: Team Group agrees to $1.1 million DRAM settlement in another false advertising lawsuit — claimed advertised memory speeds required BIOS tweaks and overclocking settings
history.description: Team Group denies all wrongdoing but has agreed to settle the lawsuit involving advertised RAM performance and overclocking-related settings.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter PC memory manufacturer Team Group has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit to the tune of $1.1 million over allegations it advertised deceptive speeds for its DDR3, DDR4, and DDR5 memory products purchased in the U.S. between May 3, 2020, and April 8, 2026. Similar to a recent case involving G.Skill, the lawsuit claims that consumers were led to believe that the advertised speeds on Team Group’s memory kits could be achieved out of the box without requiring BIOS tweaks or overclocking profiles.It was alleged by the plaintiffs that the company marketed its RAM kits using rated speeds that could only be achieved after enabling XMP or EXPO memory profiles via the motherboard BIOS/UEFI menu. Team Group has denied all allegations of wrongdoing and says that its “products were appropriately labeled and performed as represented.”An individual can claim as part of the settlement class if they purchased Team Group DDR3, DDR4, or DDR5 memory products while living in the United States between May 3, 2020, and April 8, 2026. The settlement only applies to individual consumers, while purchases made directly by a business, such as a company, LLC, corporation, or partnership, do not qualify for compensation. However, if you personally bought the memory as an individual consumer, you may still qualify even if you later used the product for work or business purposes.Latest Videos From As there is no fixed amount out of the total settlement fund of $1.1 million, the money will be divided among every individual who submits a valid claim. Payments will be made based on how many eligible Team Group DRAM products each person claims. The settlement also clarifies that one may claim compensation for up to five memory products per household without providing proof of purchase, while claims beyond that require supporting documentation.Eligible customers can head to the Claim Hub website and follow the instructions carefully to file an official claim. You can also submit an objection or exclude oneself from the lawsuit completely. The deadline for the claim is currently set for July 7, 2026.Earlier this year, G.Skill settled a similar $2.4 million lawsuit involving its DDR4 and DDR5 memory products. Plaintiffs argued that advertised speeds above JEDEC defaults required additional BIOS adjustments and overclocking settings that were not clearly disclosed on packaging or product pages. Most memory kits for modern PC platforms ship with conservative default speeds based on JEDEC standards, while higher advertised frequencies, such as DDR5-6000 or DDR5-7200, require users to manually enable XMP or EXPO profiles. The lawsuit argued that average consumers may not realize these additional steps are necessary to achieve the marketed performance levels.G.Skill was also required to change its packaging and be clearer about overclocking and BIOS adjustments as part of its agreement. The Team Group settlement doesn't seem to include any such provisions and is rather purely offering a payout to affected buyers.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Kunal Khullar
history.titleZh: Team Group同意支付110万美元和解另一起DRAM虚假诉讼——声称广告中的内存速度需要BIOS调整和超频设置
history.descriptionZh:
电脑内存制造商Team Group已同意支付110万美元，以和解一项集体诉讼。该诉讼指控其在2020年5月3日至2026年4月8日期间，在美国销售的DDR3、DDR4和DDR5内存产品上，对产品速度进行了虚假宣传。类似此前G.Skill的案例，原告认为消费者被误导，以为Team Group内存套件所标称的速度无需进行BIOS调整或启用超频配置文件即可直接实现。原告声称，该公司宣传的额定速度只有通过在主板BIOS/UEFI菜单中启用XMP或EXPO内存配置文件后才能达到。Team Group否认所有不当行为指控，并声称其“产品标签适当，性能符合描述”。

根据和解条款，任何在2020年5月3日至2026年4月8日期间居住在美国并购买了Team Group DDR3、DDR4或DDR5内存产品的个人消费者均可提出索赔。和解仅适用于个人消费者，企业（如公司、有限责任公司、合伙企业等）的直接购买行为不具赔偿资格。但若以个人消费者身份购买内存，即使之后用于工作或商业目的，仍可能符合条件。由于110万美元的和解总金额并非固定分配，而是根据每位提交有效索赔的消费者及其购买的合格DRAM产品数量进行分摊。每户家庭最多可对五件内存产品提出索赔而无需提供购买凭证，超出部分则需要提供相关证明文件。

符合条件的消费者可访问索赔中心网站，按照说明提交正式申请。也可以提交异议或完全退出诉讼。当前索赔截止日期为2026年7月7日。

今年早些时候，G.Skill曾就类似指控以240万美元达成和解，涉及DDR4和DDR5内存产品。原告声称，标称速度高于JEDEC标准的内存产品需要额外的BIOS调整和超频设置，而这些并未在产品包装或页面上明确说明。现代PC平台的大多数内存套件出厂时默认采用基于JEDEC标准的保守速度，而更高的标称频率（如DDR5-6000或DDR5-7200）需要用户手动启用XMP或EXPO配置文件。诉讼指出，普通消费者可能并未意识到需要额外步骤才能达到宣传的性能水平。作为和解协议的一部分，G.Skill还被要求更改产品包装，更清晰地说明超频和BIOS调整的必要性。而Team Group的和解协议似乎未包含此类条款，仅为受影响的买家提供经济赔偿。

history.title: Samsung and SK Hynix employees are reportedly abandoning overseas training programs to nab up to $400,000 performance bonuses — online dating grades rise as female members 'seeking out SK hynix employees'
history.description: Samsung and SK Hynix employees are reportedly considering terminating overseas training programs to be eligible for performance bonuses as AI-driven semiconductor profits fuel record bonus projections worth hundreds of millions of won.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Samsung and SK Hynix employees are reportedly rejecting overseas training programs in favor of bonuses while seeing their matchmaking grades rise on online dating sites. According to the Korean Media outlet Chosun, discussions on Samsung Electronics’ internal bulletin board indicate that employees are considering choosing potential bonuses over hard-earned, typically coveted training programs. The sentiments come amid projections that the company plans to distribute “record-high” performance bonuses due to profit surges from the AI-driven semiconductor boom, with the company’s policies restricting eligibility.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowAccording to the report, Samsung Electronics has exempted employees currently undergoing overseas training from bonus eligibility. This stance is reportedly seeing a number of employees terminating their training to return to their roles. Samsung runs a highly sought-after training program that provides employees around their 7th year at the company with financial and operational support to pursue postgraduate degrees at prestigious institutions both in and outside Korea. For example, in addition to their annual salaries, the company provides employees pursuing an MBA in the US with 500 million Korean won (~$333,000) in tuition and living expenses over two years. The support varies across institutions and countries.The program is typically highly competitive, with only 1 in 70 applicants succeeding. Now, following projections that the electronics giant could distribute an average of 600 million (~$400,000)won in bonuses to employees in the semiconductor division, interest in the training programs has dropped. Reports suggest that even those currently undergoing training are considering halting midway to be eligible for performance bonuses, as these are likely to outweigh the financial benefits of the training program.Latest Videos From You may like Every SK hynix employee could receive $477,000 bonuses this year, almost $900,000 next year Samsung chip workers reject $340,000 one-time bonus, demand annual payouts like SK hynix's $900,000 More than 30,000 Samsung union members take to the streets to demand higher cut of AI profits, an average of $400,000 per worker According to the Chosun report, sources from Samsung Electronics stated, “There have been inquiries to the HR team about whether trainees can withdraw mid-program and if they must repay previously received company support.” indicating that despite potential repayment obligations, some employees are willing to return regardless.These reports are coming even as Samsung Electronics employees are planning industrial action to compel the company to extend continuous performance bonuses to employees outside the semiconductor division. The company says only employees in the semiconductor division will receive ongoing bonuses, while others in other divisions will receive a one-time payment. The strike is scheduled for the 21st of May.At Samsung’s competitor, SK Hynix, similar reports of employees abandoning training in favor of bonuses are emerging. Despite the fact that SK Hynix takes a softer stance by offering partial bonuses to training beneficiaries, anonymous posts on its internal board reportedly include comments like, “If next year’s bonus reaches 700 million Korean won (~$466,000), not abandoning the training might make me a sudden pauper overnight” and “Seeing the projected bonuses for next and the following year, I want to cut off the hand that applied for the training.”These comments follow projections of even bigger bonuses in the coming years, as SK Hynix paid out 140 million Korean won (~$93,000) in performance bonuses early this year, which, unlike at Samsung, was distributed across the entire company. Analysts predict a dip in the popularity of overseas training programs at Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix over the next few years, as the appeal of performance bonuses grows. SK hynix bonuses are tipped to hit $477,000 this year and as much as $900,000 next year.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsBeyond strikes, life-changing decisions, and financial gain, these massive performance bonuses stemming from AI-driven profit surges are influencing quite unexpected areas of life in South Korea. Reports from Chosun indicate that SK Hynix employees are ranking higher on matchmaking companies’ eligibility scales due to bonuses and increased income, with employees reportedly being flooded with blind-date offers."In the past, if we matched a partner for an SK hynix employee at about a B+ grade, now it's unconditionally 'A-grade.' As the overwhelming bonus system has become known, we're seeing a trend where female members are seeking out SK hynix employees first,” said Son Dong-gyu, CEO of the matchmaking company Bien Aller.However, on the flip side, because bonuses are paid in direct proportion to days worked, there has been an uptick in aversion to taking extended leave, such as parental leave, as these are reportedly perceived as forfeiting income. These reports all underscore the massive influence of the AI boom on almost every aspect of humanity.
